primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable recognised in consolidated financial statements can be designated in hedge accounting under IFRS.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether intragroup items can qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements and set the specific foreign-currency exception.
This paragraph directly explains when exchange differences on intragroup monetary items remain in consolidated financial statements rather than being fully eliminated.

supporting_authority.reason: These provisions frame the main hedge accounting models and the profit-or-loss treatment of gains and losses on financial assets and liabilities relevant to FX-risk hedging.
This Interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in a net investment in a foreign operation.
IAS 21 distinguishes exchange differences recognised in profit or loss from those recognised in OCI in net-investment circumstances, which helps differentiate hedge models.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs concern translation, presentation and disclosures rather than identification of hedge accounting approaches for the item in question.
These provisions address scope, derecognition, designation at FVTPL, and other measurement matters that do not determine the peer hedge accounting models for this issue.
IAS 32 addresses classification, presentation and offsetting of financial instruments, not the hedge accounting model for FX risk on intragroup dividends.
This Interpretation concerns distributions of non-cash assets to owners and does not govern hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivables.
These are reduced-disclosure and other disclosure provisions, not the source of the hedge accounting approaches.
IFRS 7 provides disclosures about financial instruments and designated liabilities, not the core accounting models for the hedged item.
These requirements concern disclosures about interests in other entities, not hedge accounting for intragroup FX exposures.
This Interpretation addresses classification of co-operative members' shares and is unrelated to the issue.
IAS 7 addresses cash flow statement presentation and financing-liability reconciliations, not hedge accounting eligibility models.
This Interpretation concerns tax-status changes and is not relevant to the hedge accounting approaches.
This Practice Statement concerns management commentary and is unrelated to the accounting issue.
This Interpretation concerns pension surplus availability and is not relevant to identifying hedge accounting approaches here.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on recognised items or forecast transactions
Net investment hedge accounting for foreign operations

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on recognised items, making fair value hedge accounting a relevant top-level model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct top-level hedge model for foreign currency risk exposures.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 and IAS 21 identify hedge accounting for net investment foreign currency risk as a separate IFRS hedge accounting model.
